name: NeverBounce
what_it_does: Email verification service that validates individual emails in real-time or processes large lists in bulk, providing bounce prediction and deliverability scores for email marketing hygiene.
use_cases: Bulk email list cleaning before sending campaigns to reduce bounce rate
Real-time email validation at signup or checkout forms via API
Scheduled email list hygiene automation for CRM databases
Integration with ESPs (Mailchimp, SendGrid) for pre-send validation
Detecting disposable/temporary email addresses in registration flows
not_for: Email sending or delivery (this is validation only)
Catch-all domain handling — NeverBounce marks these as 'unknown' like competitors
Free validation at any meaningful scale
best_when: You have large email lists to clean at regular intervals and want a simple batch API with good documentation and ESP integrations, particularly if already using Zerobounce alternatives.
avoid_when: You need real-time validation with very low latency, or you need detailed sub-status codes beyond basic valid/invalid/catch-all classification.

auth.notes: API key as query parameter or header. Single key per account. Credits consumed per verification.

pricing.paid_tiers: Credits from $0.003-0.008/verification; volume discounts for large lists
pricing.notes: More generous free tier (1,000 credits) than ZeroBounce. Per-credit pricing without monthly commitment available.

performance.rate_limits: Real-time: throttled based on account tier; bulk jobs have size limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST responses with numeric result codes. Bulk job status requires polling. Error messages are functional but not as detailed as ZeroBounce.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Single email validation is fully idempotent. Bulk job submission creates new jobs each call — track job IDs to avoid duplicate submissions.
known_agent_gotchas: Bulk job processing is async — agents must poll for completion with job_id
Webhook for bulk job completion requires HTTPS callback URL
Results use numeric codes (0-4) with text labels — agents should use text labels not assume numeric mapping
Catch-all domains return 'unknown' — no way to validate individual mailboxes on catch-all servers
Credits consumed on 'unknown' results too — this can be a source of unexpected credit consumption

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. Email addresses are PII — GDPR applies. Data not stored after validation. SOC2 Type II. Email validation results contain information about real addresses.
